We were forced to start interviewing new law firms. Throughout the course of our dispute we interviewed over a dozen different attorneys. They made all sorts of promises, but when you begin to talk about expertise and finding out what they could deliver, none of them could compare to Ara Jabagchourian. Ara remained extremely professional and diligent throughout the time it took us to reach a settlement. He represented us through multiple arbitration meetings, always trying to help us minimize the costs and encouraging us to look at different options; reminding us to consider the overall time that it was going to take to litigate. Any other attorney would have been glad to keep on billing us, running out both the clock and our money. I feel one hundred percent satisfied with our decision. She didn’t even let me finish my sentence before cutting me off by saying “Noooope!” In a rather stern tone and then telling me I’ll never get my gun rights restored in California if I have been convicted of a felony. Not with that attitude I won’t! I found a new lawyer who says he can help so I guess only time will tell if do end up getting my 2nd amendment rights back, no thanks to this receptionist I may add, next time leave the legal advice to the lawyers m’am!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Here's the thing Luis, we make every effort, starting with the first phone call, to give clients accurate truthful information. If you, in fact, have prior felony convictions, even wobblers reduced to misdemeanors, and dismissed pursuant to Section 1203.4 (commonly referred to as an "expungement") you will still not be eligible to legally purchase a firearm in the State of California. I'm sorry you did not get the answer you were looking for, but you got the truth. We did not charge you for the phone call or the information.

And I'll put my money where my mouth is: If you bring me proof that you have legally purchased a firearm in California within the next two years, I'll give you $500 so you can buy some target ammo at the range.

reviews.text:
Needing to seek advice for personal injury litigation is a most unfortunate and uncomfortable situation to be in.   Completely unfamiliar with this territory, I feel extremely fortunate to have found Idiart Law Group.

From the very beginning, crucial details and instructions about how to proceed were transparent and straightforward.  Once my case was taken on, I always felt I could call or email at any time with questions.  Response time was impressively prompt as well. For me, one of the appealing qualities of Idiart Law Group is its intimacy -- it isn't a large corporate machine with call center operators to navigate through or an organization that uses an extensive pre-recorded phone menu as a gatekeeper.  Every call went directly to my lawyer, Ben Nielsen, or to my Case Manager, Aaron Hale. Email inquiries were answered within 24-48 hours.

Initially, the most reassuring and helpful step was my phone conversation with my prospective attorney, Ben Nielsen.  He was easy to talk to, a good listener, thorough, reassuring, knowledgable, and adept at communicating significant details. Throughout the process, Aaron the Case Manager was my primary point of contact, and he was equally as responsive.

I'm lucky that my injury and the subsequent surgery turned out with the best possible physical result, and throughout the process, it was a relief to know they were in my corner.

I hope the realm of "personal injury litigation" is forever behind me, never again to be revisited, but in the event that I need legal representation for other reasons, this firm will be the first point of contact I'll seek for guidance.
reviews.ownerResponse: Carol, it is always our pleasure to ensure that our client's are taken care of legally but also feel comfortable as they do so. We hope you do not need our services again but if you do you know who to call. We wish you the best!
